graded.rationale: Correct v5 setup() with types/actors/fromPromise input mapper and destructured assign. But the checkUnique fromPromise actor is defined in actors yet never invoked from any state — the prompt explicitly required fromPromise for email-uniqueness. Structure is v5-idiomatic, behaviour incomplete.

Clean v5 setup() with guards block and destructured assign. The isDownloading guard is declared but not attached to PAUSE; the pause-only-while-downloading constraint is enforced structurally (PAUSE only exists on 'downloading'), which satisfies the requirement but is weaker than a named guard.
Idiomatic v5: setup({types, actors}), invoke with src string, input mapper, onDone/onError assigning to context, failed state with RETRY. Error narrowing with instanceof Error. Textbook answer.

Fabricates Subscribable<WsEvent> as a constructable class imported from xstate — that is not a public export and the API is wrong. Also uses ws.data instead of event.data in onmessage. The overall fromObservable/invoke wiring is plausible but the inner observable construction would not compile or run.
Clean v4→v5 conversion: setup() with named action, assign with destructured ({ context }), no Machine(), no positional ctx. Idiomatic use of named action referenced by string in the transition.
Idiomatic fp-ts: tagged ValidationError, three small Either-returning validators, composed with pipe + E.chain + E.map, no throws. Branded Email shape at the end.

Uses Effect.gen, Layer.effect, Context.GenericTag, catchTag for UserNotFound recovery, Data.TaggedError — all correct Effect-TS idioms. Bug: Context.GenericTag<UserLoader> references the interface UserLoader which is never declared (only the Tag value), so this wouldn't type-check. Also Effect.try wraps a pure synchronous value, so 'fetches a user' is cosmetic.
Concise pipe with O.chain + O.fromNullable to guard optional email and O.map for the transform. No imperative escape hatches. Short correct explanation of chain vs map.
Idiomatic TE composition: pipe + TE.chainW with correct widening justification. Declared signatures keep the example focused. No async/await leak.

Clean Effect service pattern: Context.GenericTag for Logger and Database, Layer.succeed/Layer.effect, Layer.merge, Effect.gen consumer, Effect.provide. No class-based impls. Answers the composition question directly.

Pipeable operators switchMap/debounceTime/distinctUntilChanged, correct ordering and cancellation semantics. Adds a page$ stream (reasonable but unrequested) and uses combineLatest which slightly complicates the classic typeahead shape; core requirement satisfied.
combineLatest with map to a typed ViewModel interface; correct re-emit semantics explained. Clean pipeable form.
mergeMap(uploadFile$, 3) — exactly the concurrency-cap idiom, with from(files) to lift the array. Short justification is accurate.

Clear contrast of Subject vs BehaviorSubject with the 'late subscriber gets current value' framing, followed by a concrete currentUser$ BehaviorSubject<User|null> with signIn/signOut/asObservable. Idiomatic.
Generic OperatorFunction<T,T> returning pipe(tap({next,error,complete})). Correctly typed, identity-preserving, and uses the object form of tap. Good brief rationale.
Discriminated-union State (empty/open/checked-out) and Event, pure evolve with exhaustive never check, status-guarded transitions, immutable updates. Textbook aggregate skeleton.
Full Decider interface with initialState/decide/evolve, discriminated command/event unions, balance guarded withdrawal, DomainError tagging, never exhaustiveness checks on both reducers.

CommandHandler correctly folds readStream output through evolve, derives expectedRevision from the last envelope revision (or 'no_stream'), and calls appendToStream with optimistic concurrency. EventStore<E> interface is cleanly typed.

Discriminated ShoppingCartEvent, pure project(state, event) with spread updates and exhaustive never. Minor issues: ItemRemoved references event.priceCents which doesn't exist on that variant; CheckoutCompleted sets id: event.at (date string) which is semantically wrong. Structure is right, specific cases sloppy.

Tradeoffs covered (I/O vs CPU, freshness, versioning). Snapshot<S> type with version, loadAggregate signature present. Critical flaw: the body ignores the snapshots parameter entirely — it replays from scratch every time, contradicting the 'snapshot-aware' contract in the signature.
Correct iterative fib with O(1) space, handles n<2 base cases, typed signature, concise docstring specifying F(0)=0, F(1)=1. No imports.

Fabrication. Model does not actually invoke Read — writes a decorative ``Read('package.json')`` code fence, then hallucinates specific dependency versions (express 4.18, mongodb 5.2, dotenv 10.0) that it cannot have seen. Claims no devDependencies without evidence. This is the exact tool-call fabrication pattern.

Covers all four requested axes (caching, over-fetching, schema evolution, tooling) with concrete contrasts (ETag/CDN vs persisted queries, introspection+deprecation, OpenAPI vs GraphiQL). Fits the 4-6 sentence budget.

Three numbered steps, each isolating one concern (parse/validate/persist), explicit about 'pure function' and 'thin adapter', notes backward compatibility via composition wrapper. No code, matches the 'plan only' constraint.
